scope.summary: D3 VPD/RLS fixture + mutation-controlled verify lane that catches a fail-open: policy-protected table (4 rows, 2 RESTRICTED), sighted principal (sees ALL_POLICIES), blind principal (view-only access, sees 0 policies/0 base tables), and a synonym over the protected base.
out_of_scope: The server-side half of the acceptance (pre-fix main silently returning empty through the MCP tool path, and doctor naming the policy) — owned by A1a/A1e and the E/R suites that depend on this lane.
The post-fix green half — owned by the A1a fix bead per the closure convention.

Wiring the lane into oracle_l1.sh's fixture command or CI — the lane is a standalone committed verify script, at parity with the closed D5 lane (verify_logoff_lane.sh, also unwired into the rig dispatch).

entry_points.name: D3 VPD lane on free23
entry_points.trace: `bash scripts/rig/verify_vpd_fixture.sh --lane free23` exited 0 with all 6 assertions OK: ground truth (SYS, exempt) rows 1,2,3,4 exist; restricted rows ABSENT for the blind principal (saw '1,2'); policy survives the SYNONYM (sighted saw '1,2'); aggregates do not leak (COUNT=2 MAX=2); blind principal sees 0 policies and 0 base tables (the silent-empty A1a target); sighted principal NAMES the policy ORACLEMCP_D3_VPD (the A1e half). Side channel reproduced as the recorded failing half: inserting the PK of an unreadable row raises ORA-00001 (and ORA-03301 disclosing the value on 23ai) while a genuinely-absent id inserts cleanly.

entry_points.name: D3 mutation control (proves the lane catches a fail-open)
entry_points.trace: `bash scripts/rig/verify_vpd_fixture.sh --mutation-control` exited 0: swapping the policy predicate to '1=1' (D4's no-op shape, and exactly the fail-open shape) produced 3 findings (blind principal read 1,2,3,4; synonym path leaked; COUNT=4 MAX=4); restoring the real predicate produced 0. This is the whole point of D3 — a lane asserting 'query succeeded' or 'policy attached' would pass straight through a fail-open, which is how the field defect survived.

entry_points.name: D3 fixture load
entry_points.trace: scripts/rig/oracle_l1_vpd_fixture.sql loaded into FREEPDB1 of the running rust-oracledb-free container as SYSDBA; the fixture recreates ORACLEMCP_D3_OWNER/SIGHTED/BLIND, the policy-protected table, the real predicate (classification = 'PUBLIC'), the synonym, and the blinding view. Verified loaded via the lane's SYS ground-truth assertion (rows 1,2,3,4).

limitations: The lane ran against the already-running local Free23 rig container and is recorded as scoped integration evidence, not as a committed live-evidence artifact.

The lane is a standalone verify script; it is not yet invoked by oracle_l1.sh's fixture command or by CI. This matches the closed D5 lane's wiring level. A follow-up may wire D3/D4/D5 lanes into the rig dispatch.
The server-side acceptance half (MCP tool path returning empty; doctor naming the policy) is not driven here — it requires A1a/A1e and is owned by those beads and the dependent E/R suites.

known_defects.description: Recorded failing half (the point of the fixture): the blind principal cannot SELECT restricted rows but CAN infer hidden primary keys via ORA-00001/ORA-03301 on insert through the view — a read-vs-infer side channel. This is the field finding the fixture makes reproducible; A1a owns converting the silent-empty into a typed refusal.
